introduction: The Government of Rajasthan processes online marriage registrations via the Pehchan portal. Proper documentation guarantees the safeguarding of spouses' rights efficiently under law.
quickFacts:
Important to Know
  • Processing Time: 10-15 processing days post submission.
  • Application Fee: Standard registration fee ranges from ₹10 to ₹50, subject to e-Mitra additional charges.
  • A Marriage Certificate is valid for a lifetime.
documentsRequired:
Documents Required for Marriage Registration

Note: You need to provide documents for Proof of Age, Proof of Address, Marriage Proof, and Witnesses.
Proof of Identity & Age
  • Birth Certificate
  • SSC mark sheet
  • Aadhaar Card
Proof of Address
  • Voter ID
  • Recent electricity bills
  • Ration Card (Bhamashah/Jan Aadhaar)
Evidence of Marriage & Affidavit
  • Group and couple photos
  • Original wedding invitation card
  • Prescribed joint/single affidavit swearing the marriage took place
Witnesses
  • Details of two to three witnesses
  • Witness ID photocopies
onlineApplication:
Method 1: Apply Online via Pehchan Portal

Rajasthan's Pehchan Portal (Civil Registration System) is the official online platform for marriage registration, managed by the Economic and Statistics Department.

Step-by-Step Process:
  1. Visit Pehchan Portal
    Go to pehchan.raj.nic.in. This is Rajasthan's official civil registration portal.
  2. Select 'Aamjan' (Citizen) Section
    Click on "Aamjan" from the top navigation. Then choose "Fill Application Form" → "Marriage".
  3. Start New Application
    Click "New Application". Fill in the marriage details: names of bride and groom (as per Aadhaar), marriage date, place, district, tehsil, religion, and details of 2 witnesses.
  4. Upload Documents
    Upload scanned JPG/PDF copies: Aadhaar of both spouses, age proof (10th marksheet/Birth Certificate), address proof, wedding invitation card, ceremony photo, and joint wedding photo.
  5. Pay Fee & Submit
    Pay the nominal fee online via Rajasthan e-Payment gateway. Note your unique token/reference number and print the acknowledgement receipt.
  6. Attend Registrar Office
    Visit the local SDM/Registrar Office with all originals on the appointed date. Both spouses and witnesses must appear in person for oath and signature.
  7. Certificate Issued
    After verification, the digitally signed Marriage Certificate is uploaded to Pehchan Portal. Download it using your token number.
Tip: You can also apply via the e-Mitra Portal or visit any e-Mitra kiosk across Rajasthan for assisted online application.
offlineApplication:
Method 2: Apply Offline at e-Mitra Kiosk or Registrar Office

Visit your nearest e-Mitra kiosk or directly visit the local SDM / Registrar Office in the area where the marriage was solemnized. Working hours: Monday – Saturday, 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM.

Step-by-Step Process:
  1. Find an e-Mitra Kiosk or Registrar Office
    Locate the nearest e-Mitra kiosk at emitra.rajasthan.gov.in. You may also go directly to the SDM office of the marriage's jurisdiction.
  2. Collect the Application Form
    Request the Marriage Registration Form at the e-Mitra kiosk or Registrar office counter. The operator will help fill it in English or Hindi.
  3. Fill All Required Details
    Enter: full names (as per Aadhaar), marriage date and place, district, tehsil, religion, and full details (name, address, Aadhaar) of 2 witnesses.
  4. Notarize Affidavits
    Get a joint affidavit notarized before an Executive Magistrate or Notary declaring marital status, date and place of marriage, nationality, and religion. This is mandatory for marriages registered after 1 year.
  5. Attach Self-Attested Copies
    Attach: Aadhaar of both spouses, age proof (10th marksheet/Birth Certificate), address proof, wedding invitation, ceremony photo, joint wedding photo, and 2 witness ID proofs. Bring originals.
  6. Submit & Pay Fee
    Hand over all documents to the e-Mitra operator or registrar counter. Pay the processing fee in cash (typically ₹100–₹250). Collect the Acknowledgement Receipt with your reference/token number.
  7. Attend Registrar's Office for Verification
    Both spouses and 2 witnesses appear before the Registrar/SDM. All parties sign the marriage register. Certificate is available on Pehchan Portal after sign-off.
Helpline: Rajasthan e-Mitra Helpline: 181. Processing time: 10–15 working days post verification.
afterApplying:
After Registration - What Next?

Registrar Presence

Physical verification with the bride, groom, and witnesses requires their active presence before the registrar for recording oaths.

Certificate Issue

The marriage certificate can be securely downloaded in digital format from the Pehchan portal once verified and digitally signed by the registrar.
